topic: Conjunctions (And, But, Or, So, Because, Although, While)
keyPointsSummary: 'And' adds, 'But' contrasts, 'Or' shows choice, 'So' shows result
'Because' gives reason, 'Although' shows contrast/concession, 'While' shows simultaneous time or contrast
Conjunctions connect clauses without changing their tense
Common mistake: starting every sentence with 'And' or 'But' informally in writing

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं चाय और कॉफी दोनों पीता हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I drink both tea and coffee.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं थका हुआ था, लेकिन मैंने काम पूरा किया।
quickQuiz.answer: I was tired, but I finished the work.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या तुम चाय या कॉफी पीना चाहोगे?
quickQuiz.answer: Would you like tea or coffee?

quickQuiz.hindi: मुझे देर हो गई, इसलिए मैं जल्दी निकला।
quickQuiz.answer: I was late, so I left quickly.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं देर से आया क्योंकि ट्रैफिक था।
quickQuiz.answer: I came late because there was traffic.

quickQuiz.hindi: हालांकि वह थका हुआ था, वह काम करता रहा।
quickQuiz.answer: Although he was tired, he kept working.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं खाना बना रहा था जबकि वह पढ़ रही थी।
quickQuiz.answer: I was cooking while she was studying.

quickQuiz.hindi: मुझे यह पसंद है, लेकिन यह महंगा है।
quickQuiz.answer: I like this, but it is expensive.

quickQuiz.hindi: तुम अभी जा सकते हो या बाद में।
quickQuiz.answer: You can go now or later.

mustRememberRules: 'And' adds, 'But' contrasts, 'Or' shows choice, 'So' shows result
'Because' gives reason, 'Although' shows contrast/concession, 'While' shows simultaneous time or contrast
Conjunctions connect clauses without changing their tense
Common mistake: starting every sentence with 'And' or 'But' informally in writing
